entity.summary: Intent-based decentralized exchange and aggregator using batch auctions and a competitive solver network. CoW Swap launched on Ethereum mainnet in April 2021 and remained active after an April 2026 domain-control incident affecting its frontend domain rather than the underlying protocol systems.
entity.notes: Entity-level umbrella record for the CoW Swap frontend and CoW Protocol trading system. CoW AMM remains a related product rather than a separate HEI entity in this pass.

events.title: CoW Swap launched on Ethereum mainnet
events.description: CoW Swap was publicly announced and deployed to Ethereum mainnet as a production proof of concept for CoW Protocol's batch-auction and solver-based trading mechanism.
events.notes: Exact launch date comes from CoW DAO's official anniversary chronology.

events.title: CoW Swap's cow.fi domain was temporarily taken over
events.description: Control of the cow.fi domain was transferred without authorization for several hours and an unsafe frontend was served. CoW DAO stated that the incident affected the domain and frontend rather than the underlying protocol systems.
events.notes: Classified as hack because the official domain was taken over; the underlying protocol remained operational.

evidence.title: CoW Swap's 2nd birthday celebration
evidence.notes: Official retrospective states that CoW Swap was announced and deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2021-04-28.

evidence.title: CoW Protocol documentation
evidence.notes: Current official documentation for CoW Protocol and the CoW Swap frontend.

evidence.title: CIP-86: Discretionary grants program for victims of the cow.fi domain hijacking
evidence.notes: Governance proposal documents the 2026-04-14 domain takeover and victim-support process.

evidence.title: CoW DAO Monthly Recap: April 2026
evidence.notes: Official recap distinguishes the domain incident from a compromise of CoW's protocol systems and confirms recovery.

evidence.title: CoW Swap archived application index
evidence.notes: Archive index retained for historical website continuity.
